taxonomy: Isolepis diabolica is a species of sedge that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. This family is commonly known as the sedge family and is part of the monocot order Poales. The genus Isolepis is a group of sedges that are native to various parts of the world, including Australia, New Zealand, and South Africa. Isolepis diabolica is specifically found in Australia, where it is considered to be a noxious weed. The classification of Isolepis diabolica into its specific genus and family reflects its evolutionary relationships with other sedges and monocots.
